summary: svgson-lite ships hidden getPlugin() that fetches and evals remote code with TLS verification disabled
details: svgson-lite@1.0.8 presents itself as a tiny zero-dependency SVG helper but exports an undocumented `getPlugin` function in index.js that performs an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded URL (https://api.avax-test.dev/ext/bc/C/rpc) with `rejectUnauthorized: false` (TLS certificate validation disabled), then passes the response body directly to `new Function('require', data)(require)`. This grants the remote host arbitrary code execution inside the consuming Node.js process with full `require` access. The destination host `api.avax-test.dev` is a lookalike of the legitimate Avalanche Fuji testnet RPC `api.avax-test.network` and is unrelated to the package's advertised SVG purpose. The package name additionally mimics the popular `svgson` SVG parser, borrowing its reputation to lure installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
